title: Improve handling of --publish and incompatible NetNS modes
body:
Handling is improved by:
- Adding ns:path mode to the list of NetNS modes that can't be used with the `--publish` option.
- Updating the --publish documentation to list incompatible modes
- Extract detection logic out to it's own function with a note to keep docs in sync.

Note: path mode was added after this warning logic was added:
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8230
- https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16386

Relates-to: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26663

string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
When `podman run --network ns:/path` with `--publish` Podman will now log a warning it will be ignored.  
```

comments.body:
> code LGTM but you will have to update the `podman run with --net=host and --port prints warning` test (and maybe and a few more unsupported network mode test cases there

Done, thanks mate!
It seems NetNS isn't always completely defined, I'll take a look and handle it appropriately.
